tagline: A quiet week across the Omarchy ecosystem — mostly upstream work and open conversations.
editionNote: 26 commits across seven repos, no releases or merged PRs from @DHH.

articles.headline: bluetooth audio still hunting for the right sink on Wayland
articles.deck: *@DHH's ecosystem sits on open PRs while the community debugs Wayland audio switching and display sleep issues.*
articles.body: The omarchy project carries five open feature PRs this week, none merged. The most substantial is @purrsec's #5090, which tackles Bluetooth audio sink switching on Wayland — auto-move streams between sinks and preserve volume across switches. Wayland's audio plumbing has proven tricky; separate issues track display sleep blocking under Chromium 146 and SDDM Wayland mode stability. The work is live in PRs but not yet in the main branch.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a wireless speaker icon with dotted signal lines splitting into two paths, one fading away

articles.headline: package mirror sync ticks over nine commits for the week
articles.deck: *Routine AUR synchronization with no user-facing changes.*
articles.body: The omarchy-pkgs repository logged nine commits across the week, driven largely by the automated edge sync PR #59 opened by github-actions. No merged PRs, no releases. The repo continues to track open requests for additional Elephant Providers packages and cursor auto-update disabling.

articles.headline: minimal terminal project ticks through seven commits
articles.deck: *omaterm accumulates work without visible releases or merged changes.*
articles.body: omaterm logged seven commits this week with no releases or merged PRs. An open issue requests default Omatern skill inclusion, similar to the setup flow in omarchy itself.

articles.headline: lumon neovim theme sees single commit
articles.body: One commit landed in lumon.nvim with no additional activity reported.

articles.headline: iso installer bumps one commit amid signature and hostname questions
articles.deck: *Build tooling carries open issues around GPG key lookup and regex validation.*
articles.body: omarchy-iso recorded one commit this week. Open issues track GPG key retrieval failures during boot, hostname setup regex validation, and missing package requirements documentation — all blocking smooth first-time builds.

articles.headline: shared config repository logs two commits
articles.body: omadots registered two commits with no releases or merged PRs.

articles.headline: once-blog lands one commit
articles.deck: *The Rails-based blogging engine sees quiet week.*
articles.body: once-blog recorded a single commit with no releases or merged activity.

closingNote: 26 commits, zero releases. @DHH's repos are holding steady while the community debugs Wayland plumbing.
